reasoning: The question asks whether the CDC or another credible source will report at least 70,000 annual deaths from antibiotic-resistant infections in the US by the end of 2025. The 2019 CDC report estimated over 35,000 annual deaths. While AMR worsened during the COVID-19 pandemic (an estimated 15% increase), a jump to 70,000 (roughly doubling the 2019 CDC estimate) would require an unprecedented surge or a drastic methodological revision. Given the typical slow reporting cadence of the CDC and the magnitude of the threshold, this outcome is highly improbable by the end of 2025. The community freeze value of 0.05 reflects this strong consensus.
